sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have the full picture for Level 2:
- Pink bar (P) at row 0, full width
- White region (W) at rows 1-19, cols 0-51 (large)
- Dark gray (G) region at rows 1-63, cols 8-63 (large)
- 4 Blue blocks (b) on left edge: rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47, all at cols 0-3
- Black bar (B) at rows 20-23, cols 0-55
- White region (W) at rows 24-39, cols 0-11
- Black bar (B) at rows 40-43, cols 0-27 and 30-43
- Light green (N) segment at rows 40-43, cols 28-29
- Blue block at rows 44-47, cols 0-3
- White region (W) at rows 44-63, cols 0-7
- Light green (N) strip at rows 52-57, cols 8-9
- Charcoal (c) blob at rows 52-57, cols 10-13
The pair is the light green strip + charcoal blob at rows 52-57, cols 8-13.
The target is the light green segment at rows 40-43, cols 28-29.
In Level 1, the pair moved horizontally (left/right) controlled by top/bottom blue blocks. Here, there are 4 blue blocks on the left edge. The pair is at the bottom of the board. The target is higher up (rows 40-43 vs 52-57) and to the right (cols 28-29 vs 8-13).
Wait, looking at the adjacency:
- [12, 13] - white region adjacent to light green strip
- [13, 14] - light green strip adjacent to charcoal blob
- [2, 13] - dark gray adjacent to light green strip
- [2, 14] - dark gray adjacent to charcoal blob
The pair is in the white region area (cols 0-7 for white, but pair is at cols 8-13). The dark gray region is at cols 8-63.
Hmm, let me think about the movement. In Level 1:
- Top blue block → moved pair RIGHT
- Bottom blue block → moved pair LEFT
Here, there are 4 blue blocks. Maybe:
- Top blue (rows 16-19) → moves pair UP or RIGHT
- Bottom blue (rows 44-47) → moves pair DOWN or LEFT
But the pair needs to move both up (from rows 52-57 to rows 40-43) and right (from cols 8-13 to cols 28-29).
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. In Level 1, the pair moved horizontally. The blue blocks were on the right edge. Here, the blue blocks are on the left edge. Maybe the movement is still horizontal but in the opposite direction?
Or maybe the movement is vertical now, since the blue blocks are stacked vertically on the left edge.
Let me try clicking the top blue block first to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Click the top blue block (rows 16-19, cols 0-3)
predict(expect_change=True, note='Testing top blue block - expect pair to move')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 2}])

print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("\n=== SMALL OBJECTS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
else:
    print("Level not yet complete")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
